title: Fix timeframe display bug and results screen layout matching reference design

requirements.summary: Fix the timeframe display bug in the Results screen: the 'Time Frame' tile must display the user-selected timeframe (M1, M3, or M5) instead of always defaulting to M1. Update the countdown timer initialization to match the selected timeframe exactly (M1=60s, M3=180s, M5=300s). Compute the 'Entrar às HH:MM' entry time based on the selected timeframe duration.
acceptanceCriteria: When user selects M5 in Settings, Results screen shows 'M5' in the Time Frame tile
When user selects M3 in Settings, Results screen shows 'M3' in the Time Frame tile
When user selects M1 in Settings, Results screen shows 'M1' in the Time Frame tile
Countdown timer starts at 300 seconds (05:00) when M5 is selected
Countdown timer starts at 180 seconds (03:00) when M3 is selected
Countdown timer starts at 60 seconds (01:00) when M1 is selected
'Entrar às HH:MM' label shows correct entry time based on selected timeframe duration

file_operations.description: Modify the Results component to read the actual timeframe value from analysisResult.timeframe (which comes from localStorage via localCandleAnalysis). Update the countdown timer initialization logic to use a timeframe-to-seconds map: M1=60, M3=180, M5=300. Update the 'Time Frame' tile display to show the correct timeframe value from analysisResult.timeframe instead of hardcoded 'M1'. Compute the entry time ('Entrar às') by adding the timeframe duration in seconds to the current time at page load. Ensure the 'TEMPO' tile displays the countdown in MM:SS format with 'restante' label, and 'ENTRAR ÀS' tile displays the entry time in HH:MM format in cyan/teal with 'horário de entrada' label.

requirements.summary: Fix the timeframe propagation in localCandleAnalysis.ts: read the user's selected timeframe from localStorage at analysis execution time (not at module load time). Ensure the localStorage key matches exactly the key used by Settings.tsx. Store the retrieved value ('M3', 'M5', or 'M1') verbatim in the result.timeframe field, defaulting to 'M1' only if no value exists.
acceptanceCriteria: localCandleAnalysis.ts reads timeframe from localStorage at call time, not at import time
The localStorage key used in localCandleAnalysis.ts matches exactly the key used in Settings.tsx to persist the timeframe
result.timeframe is 'M5' when user had M5 selected in Settings
result.timeframe is 'M3' when user had M3 selected in Settings
result.timeframe is 'M1' when user had M1 selected in Settings
result.timeframe defaults to 'M1' only if no value is found in localStorage

file_operations.description: Modify the analyzeChartLocally function to read the timeframe value from localStorage at execution time (inside the function body) instead of at module load. Identify the correct localStorage key by cross-referencing Settings.tsx (likely 'defaultTimeframe' or 'userSettings.defaultTimeframe'). Parse the stored value and assign it to result.timeframe verbatim ('M1', 'M3', or 'M5'). If no value is found or the key does not exist, default to 'M1'. Ensure this change does not affect any other parts of the analysis logic.

requirements.summary: Update the Results screen layout to match the reference design (image-11.png, image-12.png). Display two side-by-side tiles at the top: 'TEMPO' countdown (left) and 'ENTRAR ÀS' entry time (right). Show a full-width green banner for COMPRA (red for VENDA) with Tendência, Confiança percentage, and Força badge inline. Display a 2×2 info grid (Tendência, Precisão, Volume, Time Frame). Show the chart thumbnail below the grid as a scrollable secondary section with support/resistance overlays.
acceptanceCriteria: Top section shows two dark tiles side by side: TEMPO countdown and ENTRAR ÀS time
TEMPO tile displays MM:SS countdown in large bold white text with 'restante' label below
ENTRAR ÀS tile displays entry time in cyan/teal with 'horário de entrada' label below
Signal banner is full-width green for COMPRA, red for VENDA
Signal banner displays Tendência, Confiança percentage, and Força badge inline
2×2 info grid shows Tendência, Precisão, Volume, and Time Frame tiles
Time Frame tile reflects the user-selected timeframe (M1/M3/M5)
Layout matches the reference screenshots (image-11.png, image-12.png)

file_operations.description: Restructure the Results component layout to match the reference design. At the top, create a flex container with two equally-sized dark tiles: left tile shows 'TEMPO' label, countdown timer in large bold white (e.g., '00:29'), and 'restante' subtitle; right tile shows 'ENTRAR ÀS' label, entry time in cyan/teal (e.g., '19:10'), and 'horário de entrada' subtitle. Below that, render a full-width signal banner (green for COMPRA, red for VENDA) displaying '↗ COMPRA' or '↘ VENDA', 'Tendência: ALTA/BAIXA', 'Confiança: XX%', and a 'Força: forte/média/fraca' badge inline within the banner. Below the banner, create a 2×2 grid of dark tiles showing: (1) Tendência with colored arrow, (2) Precisão with value, (3) Volume with value, (4) Time Frame with the selected timeframe (M1/M3/M5). Below the grid, display the chart thumbnail as a scrollable secondary section using the ChartOverlay component with support/resistance overlays. Apply dark theme styling, rounded corners, and spacing matching the reference screenshots. Ensure all labels are in Portuguese.

requirements.summary: Fix the Settings page timeframe selector to correctly persist the selected value (M1, M3, or M5) to localStorage and to the backend via updateSettings mutation. Audit the localStorage key name to ensure it matches the key read by localCandleAnalysis.ts. Show the Portuguese success toast 'Configuração salva!' after saving. Verify that the saved value persists across navigation.
acceptanceCriteria: Selecting M5 in Settings and saving stores 'M5' in localStorage under the correct key
Selecting M3 in Settings and saving stores 'M3' in localStorage under the correct key
Navigating away and returning to Settings shows the previously selected timeframe
Toast 'Configuração salva!' appears after each successful save
The localStorage key in Settings.tsx exactly matches the key read in localCandleAnalysis.ts

file_operations.description: Modify the Settings component to ensure the timeframe selector (radio buttons or select input for M1/M3/M5) correctly persists the selected value to localStorage and to the backend via the updateSettings mutation from useUpdateSettings. Audit the localStorage key used (likely 'defaultTimeframe' or within a 'userSettings' object) and ensure it matches exactly the key read by localCandleAnalysis.ts. When the user selects a timeframe and saves, write the selected value ('M1', 'M3', or 'M5') to localStorage and call the mutation. On successful save, display a toast with the Portuguese message 'Configuração salva!'. Ensure the component reads the current value from localStorage or the settings query on mount, so returning to Settings displays the previously saved value. Verify the timeframe is included in the UserSettings payload sent to the backend.

requirements.summary: Ensure the analysis explanation text (explicacao field) generated by localCandleAnalysis.ts is unique per analysis and meaningfully references the specific detected patterns, trend direction, candle characteristics, confidence score, and momentum count. Prevent generic or repetitive phrases by incorporating computed values into the explanation template dynamically.
acceptanceCriteria: Two different chart images analyzed back-to-back produce different explicacao texts
The explicacao text references the specific detected pattern names (e.g., 'Engolfo de Alta', 'Martelo')
The explicacao text references the detected trend direction (ALTA/BAIXA/LATERAL)
The explicacao text references the computed confidence or momentum value
The explicacao text uses professional Portuguese trading analyst language
No hardcoded static string is returned regardless of input — the text varies with analysis results

file_operations.description: Modify the analyzeChartLocally function to generate a dynamic, unique explanation text (explicacao field) for each analysis. Instead of returning a static or generic string, construct the explicacao using a template that incorporates the computed values: detected pattern names (e.g., 'Engolfo de Alta', 'Martelo', 'Doji'), trend direction ('ALTA', 'BAIXA', 'LATERAL'), confidence score, momentum count, and any other relevant computed metrics. Use professional Portuguese trading analyst language. For example: 'A análise identificou um padrão de [pattern name] com tendência [ALTA/BAIXA/LATERAL]. A confiança da análise é de [confidence]% com força [forte/média/fraca]. Foram detectados [momentum count] momentos de alta pressão compradora/vendedora.' Ensure each analysis produces a unique text by varying the content based on the actual detected features in the chart.
